title: Reckoning With the Evolving China-Russia Threat
gist: Christopher Walker argues the China-Russia-North Korea-Iran military entanglement around the Ukraine war has moved far beyond what analysts thought plausible a decade ago, from Chinese economic/logistical support for Moscow's war effort to reported secret Chinese training of Russian forces in radiological, biological, and chemical warfare.

insights.claim: China now functions as the economic and logistical lifeline enabling Russia to wage full-scale war against Ukraine, a role that would have seemed implausible to most observers as recently as 2016.
insights.speaker: Christopher Walker
insights.so_what: Quantifies the depth of China-Russia wartime entanglement, relevant to sanctions and export-control risk on Chinese entities tied to Russia's military supply chain.
insights.locator: In 2016, if an analyst had suggested

insights.claim: China, Russia, and Iran are cooperating to mass-produce lethal drones that have killed thousands of Ukrainians.
insights.speaker: Christopher Walker
insights.so_what: Identifies a trilateral China-Russia-Iran military-industrial supply chain as an active target for sanctions enforcement and interdiction.
insights.locator: if that same analyst had added that Beijing and Moscow

insights.claim: North Korean troops are fighting alongside Russian forces in Europe, gaining direct battlefield experience and lessons in modern warfare.
insights.speaker: Christopher Walker
insights.so_what: North Korea is accumulating battle-tested military expertise from the Ukraine war, a factor to weigh in Korean-peninsula and broader East Asian security-risk assessments.
insights.locator: The notion that North Korean troops would be fighting

insights.claim: Chinese leadership has reportedly provided Russian forces with secret military training, including on 'radiological, biological, and chemical warfare,' according to Reuters reporting from July 1, 2026.
insights.speaker: Christopher Walker
insights.so_what: Marks an escalation from economic support into direct Chinese military-technical training of Russian forces on NBC (nuclear/biological/chemical) warfare — a material proliferation and escalation risk to monitor.
